title: Setup vs Hold Cheat Sheet
content_markdown: Setup = max-delay (`Tclk+Tskew >= Tcq+Tlogic+Tsetup`); fix with faster data or slower clock. Hold = min-delay (`Tcq+Tlogic >= Thold+Tskew`); fix by adding delay. Slowing the clock never fixes hold.

title: When to Use Which Synchronizer
content_markdown: Single-bit control -> 2-flop synchronizer. Multi-bit value crossing slowly -> Gray code. Control+data -> req/ack handshake. Streaming data -> async FIFO. Never put a bus through parallel 2-flop synchronizers.

title: FIFO Depth Formula
content_markdown: Depth = burst_length * (1 - read_rate/write_rate), rounded up (often to a power of two). State your burst-length and rate assumptions explicitly.

title: Async Assert, Sync De-assert
content_markdown: Preferred reset: assert asynchronously (works without a clock) and release synchronously through a 2-flop reset synchronizer to avoid recovery/removal metastability.

title: Dynamic Power Knobs
content_markdown: P_dyn = a*C*V^2*f. Voltage is quadratic (biggest knob, paired with DVFS), then activity (clock/operand gating), then frequency. Leakage needs power gating and high-Vt cells.

title: Power-Down Sequence
content_markdown: Order matters: assert isolation -> save state to retention -> turn off power switches. Reverse to power up. Getting the sequence wrong corrupts live logic or loses state.

title: Shifter vs Isolation
content_markdown: Level shifter = crossing voltage domains (esp. low-to-high). Isolation cell = clamping a powered-down domain's outputs. A net may need both; isolation must sit on the always-on side.

title: V/F Ordering Rule
content_markdown: Speeding up: raise voltage before frequency. Slowing down: lower frequency before voltage. Transitions must be glitch-free and use defined operating performance points (OPPs).

title: Latency vs Throughput
content_markdown: Pipelining adds latency and area but raises throughput and fmax. For N items through k stages: cycles = N + k - 1. Clock period is set by the slowest stage, so balance them.

title: Forward First, Stall Last
content_markdown: Data hazards: forward results from EX/MEM or MEM/WB back to the consuming stage. Only stall (insert a bubble) when forwarding cannot cover it, e.g. the load-use hazard.

title: AMAT and the 3 Cs
content_markdown: AMAT = Hit_time + Miss_rate * Miss_penalty. Miss types: Compulsory, Capacity, Conflict. Higher associativity reduces conflict misses. Address = tag | index | offset.

title: Async FIFO Needs Gray Code
content_markdown: Cross FIFO pointers between clock domains as Gray code so only one bit changes; a metastable sample then resolves to either the old or new value, both safe. Binary pointers are not.
